info.title: Benefits Documents Service API
info.description:
The Benefits Documents API lets you:
* Submit supporting 526EZ claims documentation to a VBMSeFolder.
* Retrieve a list of supporting 526EZ claim document names from a Veteran's VBMS eFolder.
* Return upload status of documents submitted to a VBMS eFolder.
* Search for and download claims letter documents.

## Technical overview
The Benefits Documents API receives a VBMS Participant ID or File Number. The ID or File
Number is used to identify the Veteran or dependent associated with a particular VBMS eFolder.

With either the Participant ID or File Number, you can:
* Submit supporting documentation for a disability claim.
* Retrieve a list of supporting claims document names from the eFolder.
* Check the status of submitted claims documents.
* Search for and download claims letter documents.

Accepted document attachment types for submissions are:
  * PDF
  * JPG
  * TXT
  * TIF
  * PNG
  * GIF
  * BMP

The Participant ID (participantId) parameter can be used to identify Veterans and dependents.
If Veterans and dependents of deceased Veterans submit this parameter, they can upload and
search for supporting claims documentation in their associated Veteran's eFolder. 

**Note**: Dependents of living Veterans can not upload and search for documentation. 

### Authentication and authorization 
The authentication model for the Benefits Documents Service API is based on OAuth 2.0/OpenID
Connect and supports client [credentials grant (CCG)](https://developer.va.gov/explore/api/benefits-documents/client-credentials).

**Important**: To get production access, you must either work for VA or have specific VA agreements in
place. If you have questions, [contact us](https://developer.va.gov/support/contact-us).

post.summary: Returns the upload status of multiple disability claim documents.
post.description:
Returns the current processing status of multiple disability
   claims document uploads related to the given list of request IDs.

post.summary: Returns all VBMS document names associated with a participantId.
post.description: Returns a list of all VBMS document names related to participantId.

example.message: The server took too long to respond

post.summary: Download the document for a participant.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to download the full content of a document (such
  as a PDF). The document must be identified by its unique ID, and associated with
  either a Participant ID or File Number.

example.message: The server took too long to respond

post.summary: Uploads a file to a Veteran's VBMS eFolder.
post.description:
Receives and uploads a file based on the required parameters of systemName, docType,
fileNumber, participantId, claimId, and fileName. Parameters are described in
the schema section for UploadRequestV1.UploadData.

## Example Payload (using File Number)

The following demonstrates a (redacted) multipart payload, identified by fileNumber,
suitable for submitting to this endpoint. Most programming languages should have
provisions for assembling a multipart payload like this without having to do so
manually.

```
POST /benefits-documents/v1/documents HTTP/1.1
Host: <server>
User-Agent: curl/7.75.0
Accept: */*
Authorization: Bearer <token>
Content-Length: 11871
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=------------------------94408bea7cc00a68

Content-Type: application/json

{
  "data": {
    "systemName":"VA.gov",
    "docType":"L533",
    "fileNumber":"987654321",
    "claimId":"123",
    "fileName":"Test-PDF-Valid.pdf",
    "trackedItemIds": [ 456,789 ]
  }
}

```

In this curl example, we store the parameters inside JSON file called
upload-request.p2iO.json:

```
{
  "data": {
    "systemName":"VA.gov",
    "docType":"L533",
    "fileNumber":"987654321",
    "claimId":"123",
    "fileName":"Test-PDF-Valid.pdf",
    "trackedItemIds": [ 456,789 ]
  }
}

```

This is an example curl command:

```

curl -v '-HAuthorization: Bearer <token>' -HContent-Type:multipart/form-data --form 'parameters=@upload-request.p2iO.json;type=application/json' --form 'file=@<path/to/pdf/file>;type=application/pdf' https://sandbox-api.va.gov/services/benefits-documents/v1/documents

```

## Example Payload (using Participant ID)

To upload using participantId, we'll use the same upload example as above, this time
providing a participantId instead of a fileNumber:

```
POST /benefits-documents/v1/documents HTTP/1.1
Host: <server>
User-Agent: curl/7.75.0
Accept: */*
Authorization: Bearer <token>
Content-Length: 11871
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=------------------------94408bea7cc00a68

Content-Type: application/json

{
  "data": {
    "systemName":"VA.gov",
    "docType":"L533",
    "participantId":"123456789",
    "claimId":"123",
    "fileName":"Test-PDF-Valid.pdf",
    "trackedItemIds": [ 456,789 ]
  }
}

```

We can store the parameters inside a JSON file as in the previous example - this time
we'll call it upload-request-participant-id.p2iO.json.

Then, we can execute the same cURL command in the previous example,
using the new parameters file:

```

curl -v '-HAuthorization: Bearer <token>' -HContent-Type:multipart/form-data --form 'parameters=@upload-request-participant-id.p2iO.json;type=application/json' --form 'file=@<path/to/pdf/file>;type=application/pdf' https://sandbox-api.va.gov/services/benefits-documents/v1/documents

```

post.summary: Validates that the claimant can upload a file to a Veteran's VBMS eFolder.
post.description:
Determines whether a claimant, identified by either a fileNumber or participantId,
can upload documents of the given docType for the claim identified by the
provided claimId. If the claimant is successfully validated, a validation token
and token expiration time are provided. The token can be used to avoid duplicate
validation for an upload using identical parameters. Note that while a successful
response indicates the pairing of claimant, document, and claim information is
valid, it does not guarantee success of the operation itself.

post.summary:
Returns all claims evidence document names associated with a participantId
  or fileNumber.
post.description:
Returns a list of all claims evidence document names related to the
   claim identified by the claimId, fileNumber, or participantId.

example.message: The server took too long to respond

post.summary:
Returns a list of downloadable Claims Evidence claim letter documents, along with
  their associated metadata, linked to a Participant ID or File Number.
post.description:
Returns a list of downloadable Claims Evidence claim letter documents associated
  with the provided Participant ID or File Number. Each document entry includes
  metadata such as the document UUID, type, subject, original file name, tracked
  item ID, and upload date.

example.message: The server took too long to respond

post.summary: Download the full claim letter document for a claimant.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to download the full content of a claim letter document (such
  as a PDF). The document must be identified by its unique ID, and associated with
  either a Participant ID or File Number. Note: Only some document types are
  available for download.

get.description:
Returns the current processing status of a disability claims document upload
  related to the given requestId.

BasicErrorDocumentV1.description: An error response in JSON:API format (see https://jsonapi.org/format/#document-structure).

errors.description: The list of errors returned from the attempted operation.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostic information that can be submitted with a support request to aid with diagnosing and solving issues.

UploadRequestAsyncProcessingStatusBulkRe.description: Requests the asynchronous processing status of more than one document upload request.

requestIds.description: The list of requestIds that identify upload requests to retrieve status for.

requestIdsNotFound.description: A list populated with requestIds whose status was not found.

UploadRequestAsyncProcessingStatusV1.description: The asynchronous processing workflow status of a single upload request.

requestId.description: The requestId of the upload request.

status.description:
The current execution status of the workflow:
* `IN_PROGRESS`: the workflow is currently executing.
* `SUCCESS`: the workflow has completed all steps successfully.
* `FAILED`: the workflow could not complete because a step encountered
a non-recoverable error.

steps.description: The list of workflow steps that process the document upload, ordered by their sequence.

UploadRequestAsyncProcessingStatusV1.Ste.description: The description of a single workflow step.

name.description:
The workflow step name:
* `CLAIMS_EVIDENCE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Claims Evidence.
* `BENEFITS_GATEWAY_SERVICE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Benefits
Gateway Service.
* `REMOVE_FILE_BINARY`: the workflow step that removes file binary
Gateway Service.

nextStepName.description:
The workflow step name:
* `CLAIMS_EVIDENCE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Claims Evidence.
* `BENEFITS_GATEWAY_SERVICE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Benefits
Gateway Service.
* `REMOVE_FILE_BINARY`: the workflow step that removes file binary
Gateway Service.

description.description: A short description of the workflow step

status.description:
The current execution status of the workflow step:
* `NOT_STARTED`: the workflow step is not yet executing and awaiting completion
of the previous step.
* `IN_PROGRESS`: the workflow step is currently executing.
* `SUCCESS`: the workflow step has completed successfully.
* `FAILED`: the workflow step encountered a non-recoverable error.

detail.description: A human-readable summary of the error.

step.description:
The workflow step name:
* `CLAIMS_EVIDENCE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Claims Evidence.
* `BENEFITS_GATEWAY_SERVICE`: the workflow step that uploads the document to Benefits
Gateway Service.
* `REMOVE_FILE_BINARY`: the workflow step that removes file binary
Gateway Service.

startTime.description: The time, in milliseconds since epoch, that the workflow began executing.

endTime.description:
The time, in milliseconds since epoch, that the workflow finished executing.
In the case of a currently executing workflow, this will be null.

pageNumber.description: 1-based page number to retrieve. Defaults to 1.

pageSize.description: Number of results per page (1–100). Defaults to 100.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

subject.example: File contains evidence related to the claim

documentUuid.description:
This value is obtained from the uuid field from BIP Claims Evidence.
It is the same value as the documentId field
reported by the /documents/search endpoint.

currentVersionUuid.description: This value is obtained from the currentVersionUuid field from BIP Claims Evidence.

documentTypeLabel.example: VA 21-526 Veterans Application for Compensation or Pension

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

documentUuid.description:
The document's unique identifier in VBMS, obtained by making a Document Service API
request to search for documents that are available to download for the Veteran.
Note that this differs from the document's current version UUID.

trackedItemIds.description:
A list of tracked item IDs that are associated
with the document being uploaded.

systemName.description: Indicates which system is uploading the document.

docType.description: Indicates the L code of the type of document being uploaded.

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

claimId.description: The VBMS-issued claim identifier with which the document is associated.

fileName.description: The name of the file being uploaded.

validationToken.description:
An optional validation token obtained during request claimant pre-validation,
that bypasses duplicate validation calls. For more information, review the section
on request pre-validation.

success.description: Indicates if the upload request was successful.

docType.description: Indicates the L code of the type of document upload being validated.

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

claimId.description:
The VBMS-issued claim identifier with which the document upload being
validated is associated.

valid.description: Whether the claimant was validated successfully.

success.description: The validation result, populated in the case of successful validation.

error.description: The validation error, populated in the case of failed validation.

validUntil.description: The time, in milliseconds since epoch, that the token expires.

claimId.description: The VBMS-issued claim identifier with which the document is associated.

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

documentUuid.description:
The document's unique identifier in VBMS.
Note that this differs from the document's current version UUID.

currentVersionUuid.description: This value is obtained from the currentVersionUuid field from BIP Claims Evidence.

documentTypeLabel.example: VA 21-526 Veterans Application for Compensation or Pension

documents.description: The list of benefits claim documents.

docTypeIds.description:
The numeric document types to search for. If not provided, then
all downloadable claim letter documents matching the other
request criteria will be returned.

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

docTypeId.description: The numeric type code of the document.

documentUuid.description:
The document's unique identifier in VBMS.
Note that this differs from the document's current version UUID.

currentVersionUuid.description:
This value is obtained from the currentVersionUuid field from BIP Claims
Evidence.

documentTypeLabel.example: VA 21-526 Veterans Application for Compensation or Pension

documents.description: The list of claim letter documents available for download.

fileNumber.description:
The Veteran's VBMS fileNumber used when uploading the document to VBMS.
 It indicates the eFolder in which the document resides.

participantId.description:
A unique identifier assigned to each patient entry in the Master Patient Index
linking patients to their records across VA systems.

documentUuid.description:
The document's unique identifier in VBMS, obtained by making a Document Service API
request to search for documents that are available to download for the Veteran.
Note that this differs from the document's current version UUID.

scopes.documents.write: The Veteran's access to upload files that provide verification of eligibility for their benefits.
scopes.documents.read: The Veteran's access to read data about files that have been uploaded.
